query: What is the current level of crude oil in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve as of June 2026, per EIA weekly data (WCSSTUS1)? What has been the weekly trajectory over the past 3-6 months (fills or releases)?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of my findings on the U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve:

**Current Level (EIA series WCSSTUS1):** SPR crude oil stocks stood at **331.191 million barrels for the week ending June 19, 2026** — the latest available data point. That's down 9.06M bbl (-2.66%) week-over-week and down ~17.7% year-over-year.

**Weekly Trajectory (past ~6 months):** Two distinct phases in 2026:
- **Jan–March: Slow fill/plateau** — rose from ~413.5M (early Jan) to a 2026 peak of ~415.4M bbl (mid-March), then flat.
- **April–June: Sustained large releases** — draws began the week of April 3 and accelerated to **~8–10 million barrels/week** through May and June. The week ending April 24 saw a 7.1M bbl release, the largest since October 2022.

**Cumulative drawdown:** ~84 million barrels from the March peak to June 19 — a steady, accelerating release trajectory, not fills.

**Driver:** DOE releases tied to an IEA-coordinated international response to Middle East supply disruptions (U.S. share up to ~172M bbl of a ~400M bbl global effort), structured as an exchange (barrels to be returned later) rather than a permanent statutory sale.

The trajectory is clearly one of **ongoing releases**, with the reserve declining roughly 9M bbl in each of the most recent weeks.

sources.title: DOE has released 17.5 million barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve since March
sources.snippet: DOE released 17.5 million barrels between the week ending March 20 and week ending April 24; 7.1M bbl in the week ending April 24, most since Oct 7, 2022; SPR at 397.9M bbl at that time; part of IEA-coordinated exchange response to Middle East disruption.

sources.title: US Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve Stocks (Weekly)
sources.snippet: 331.19M bbl for week of Jun 19 2026, down from 340.25M last week and down 17.67% from one year ago (402.29M).
